'''Da Soocha''' was a [[Water/Legends|water]]-covered [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the uncharted [[Cyax system/Legends|Cyax system]] in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].

==History==
The [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] held the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Cyax]] in high esteem, though they never explored the [[Star system/Legends|system]] it was located in. Despite this, they spread many legends of a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] covered with an intelligent, living [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] that was warmed by Cyax's rays. Da Soocha's name translated from [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]] as literally "'''Waking Planet'''".

During the [[Galactic War]], the [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] on Da Soocha were planning a slave rebellion.<ref name="Mission">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Underworld Trading|Shattering Their Chains}}</ref>

[[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] had the coordinates for the world in [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|his palace]], and they were stolen by [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. At the time of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] explored the Cyax system and were rather surprised to find the Hutt legend to be partially truthful: it was indeed a large, ocean-covered planet. In honor of the Hutt legend, the planet was dubbed Da Soocha.

During the Reborn [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Operation Shadow Hand|campaign]] from [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] to [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], the planet was colonized by [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] refugees fleeing from the [[World Devastator]] [[Battle of Calamari|attack]] on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] also established a base on its uncharted [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Da Soocha V]], though they were forced to evacuate after Palpatine used the [[Galaxy Gun]] to destroy the moon.

[[File:Viral_spores.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The [[Devastation of Da Soocha]].]]

In [[137 ABY]], the [[First Sith Imperial Strikeforce]] under [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Rulf Yage|Yage]] and the two [[One Sith|Sith]] [[Darth Azard]] and [[Vul Isen]] unleashed a deadly toxin into oceans of Da Soocha, killing all life in the ocean world in a similar way as with [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. The Sith intended to punish the Hutt [[Azzim Anjiliac Atirue]] for harboring [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] refugees escaping the Sith's genocide at the [[Hutt Temple]] spa [[Maya Armus]] on the moon [[Napdu]].

They then obliterated the Temple and killed all the survivors who attempted to escape. However, the Azzim's [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] servant [[Xanif]] managed to transmit a recording of the Sith's actions to his uncle [[Vedo Anjiliac Atirue]] on the Hutt capital [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]]. Despite an [[Devastation of Da Soocha|Imperial victory]], this action outraged the Hutts who subsequently contacted [[Cade Skywalker]], a former Jedi and perennial foe of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]].

==Behind the scenes==
In [[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]], Da Soocha is erroneously referred to as '''Gla Soocha'''. However, the PC game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] offers "Gla Soocha" as a common alternative name for the planet.

''Rebellion'' mistakenly places Da Soocha in the [[Abrion sector/Legends|Abrion sector]].

Additionally, ''Rebellion's'' in-game encyclopedia entry for Da Soocha claims the Mon Calamari had been using the planet as an evacuation site even before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], and that the [[Da Soocha V|Pinnacle Moon]] was suspected of housing a Rebel outpost some ten years before the moon was charted. This is contradictory to its appearance in the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' series.
